questions.questionText: A cuboid has dimensions 5 cm, 8 cm, and 10 cm. Calculate its volume.
questions.explanation: Volume of a cuboid = length × width × height. So, 5 cm × 8 cm × 10 cm = 400 cm³.

questions.questionText: A cylinder has a radius of 3 cm and a height of 7 cm. Calculate its volume. Use π = 3.14.
questions.explanation: Volume of a cylinder = π × r² × h. So, 3.14 × (3 cm)² × 7 cm = 3.14 × 9 cm² × 7 cm = 197.82 cm³.

questions.questionText: A sphere has a radius of 6 cm. Calculate its volume, leaving your answer in terms of π.
questions.explanation: Volume of a sphere = (4/3) × π × r³. So, (4/3) × π × (6 cm)³ = (4/3) × π × 216 cm³ = 288π cm³.

questions.questionText: A square-based pyramid has a base side length of 6 cm and a perpendicular height of 10 cm. Calculate its volume.
questions.explanation: Volume of a pyramid = (1/3) × base area × height. Base area = 6 cm × 6 cm = 36 cm². So, (1/3) × 36 cm² × 10 cm = 120 cm³.

questions.questionText: A cone has a radius of 5 cm and a height of 12 cm. Calculate its volume, leaving your answer in terms of π.
questions.explanation: Volume of a cone = (1/3) × π × r² × h. So, (1/3) × π × (5 cm)² × 12 cm = (1/3) × π × 25 cm² × 12 cm = 100π cm³.

questions.questionText: A cuboid has a volume of 240 cm³. If its length is 8 cm and width is 5 cm, what is its height?
questions.explanation: Volume = length × width × height. So, 240 cm³ = 8 cm × 5 cm × height. 240 = 40 × height. Height = 240/40 = 6 cm.

questions.questionText: The volume of a cylinder is 300π cm³. If its height is 12 cm, what is its radius?
A hemispherical bowl has a radius of 9 cm. Calculate the volume of the bowl, leaving your answer in terms of π.
questions.explanation: Volume of a hemisphere = (1/2) × (4/3) × π × r³ = (2/3) × π × r³. So, (2/3) × π × (9 cm)³ = (2/3) × π × 729 cm³ = 486π cm³.

questions.questionText: A prism has a uniform cross-section with an area of 45 cm². If the length of the prism is 10 cm, what is its volume?
questions.explanation: Volume of a prism = cross-sectional area × length. So, 45 cm² × 10 cm = 450 cm³.

questions.questionText: A rectangular-based pyramid has a base measuring 4 cm by 7 cm and a volume of 84 cm³. What is the perpendicular height of the pyramid?
questions.explanation: Volume of a pyramid = (1/3) × base area × height. Base area = 4 cm × 7 cm = 28 cm². So, 84 cm³ = (1/3) × 28 cm² × height. 84 = (28/3) × height. Height = (84 × 3) / 28 = 9 cm.

questions.questionText: Two geometrically similar solids have volumes in the ratio 8:27. What is the ratio of their corresponding lengths?
questions.explanation: For similar solids, the ratio of volumes is the cube of the ratio of lengths. If V₁:V₂ = L₁³:L₂³, then L₁:L₂ = ³√V₁:³√V₂. So, ³√8:³√27 = 2:3.

questions.questionText: A cone has a volume of 75π cm³. If its radius is 5 cm, what is its height?
